perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement (regardless of the cause for termination); (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction (after all appeals have been taken) with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly (through, on behalf of, or in conjunction with any persons, partnership, corporation or entity).
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Post-Term Period" shall mean a continuous uninterrupted period from the date of: (a) a transfer permitted under Section 14 of the Franchise Agreement; (b) expiration or termination of the Franchise Agreement; (c) termination of Member's employment with Franchisee; and/or (d) a final order of a duly authorized arbitrator, panel of arbitrators, or a court of competent jurisdiction with respect to any of the foregoing or with respect to the enforcement of this Agreement; either directly or indirectly.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years' has been omitted. This omission introduces ambiguity regarding the duration of the 'Post-Term Period'. This contradicts the intent established by the original Franchise Agreement that it should be 2 years. The term will have to be interpreted without context.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Member covenants and agrees that during the Post-Term Period (defined below), except as otherwise approved in writing by Franchisor, Member shall not, either directly or indirectly, own, manage, engage in, be employed by, advise, make loans to, consult for, or have any other interest in any Competitive Business that is, or intends to operate, within a three (3) mile radius of the premises of your Franchised Business or within a three (3) mile radius of any Franchised Business then-operating or under construction to operate under the System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'a continuous uninterrupted period of two (2) years', which defines the 'Post-Term Period'. The contradicted text refers to obligations 'during the Post-Term Period', relying on the definition provided in the original text. Without the duration being specified in the changed text, the duration of the obligations in the contradicted text becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: All agreements and covenants contained herein are severable. If any of them, or any part or parts of them, shall be held invalid by any court of competent jurisdiction for any reason, then the Member agrees that the court shall have the authority to reform and modify that provision in order that the restriction shall be the maximum necessary to protect Franchisor's and/or Franchisee's legitimate business needs as permitted by applicable law and public policy. In so doing, the Member agrees that the court shall impose the provision with retroactive effect as close as possible to the provision held to be invalid.
perturbation.changed_text: All agreements and covenants contained herein are severable. If any of them, or any part or parts of them, shall be held invalid by any court of competent jurisdiction for any reason, then the Member agrees that the court shall have the authority to reform and modify that provision in order that the restriction shall be the maximum necessary to protect Franchisor's and/or Franchisee's legitimate business needs as permitted by applicable law and public policy.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'In so doing, the Member agrees that the court shall impose the provision with retroactive effect as close as possible to the provision held to be invalid' has been omitted. This omission introduces uncertainty regarding the retroactive effect of the provision. It is uncertain on what will happen on the period that it lasted invalid and that creates a lack of clarity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All agreements and covenants contained herein are severable. If any of them, or any part or parts of them, shall be held invalid by any court of competent jurisdiction for any reason, then the Member agrees that the court shall have the authority to reform and modify that provision in order that the restriction shall be the maximum necessary to protect Franchisor's and/or Franchisee's legitimate business needs as permitted by applicable law and public policy.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the phrase regarding the retroactive effect of the provision introduces uncertainty regarding the court's power to impose the provision retroactively, contradicting the original intent as stated in the original text that the court *shall* impose the provision with retroactive effect as close as possible to the provision held to be invalid. The change introduces ambiguity, potentially leading to disputes and making the enforcement of the provision unclear because there is no mention on what will happen during the period the provision was invalid.
